summary: Large ASM-side repository reorganization plus Makefile cleanup. The PR moved many flat asm/*.s files into subsystem folders such as asm/MSL, asm/MetroTRK, asm/Runtime, asm/dolphin/*, asm/melee/ft, asm/melee/gr, asm/melee/lb, and asm/sysdolphin/baselib, updated obj_files.mk to point at the new paths, adjusted Makefile scanning/include paths, and removed the _asm suffix from several assembly filenames where they were relocated. Most file moves were 100% similarity renames, making this primarily an organizational/build-maintenance change rather than functional decompilation work.

key_files.role: Changed source/assembly discovery from fixed directory lists to shell find commands and updated include paths for sysdolphin/baselib and melee/lb.
Updated object file paths to match the reorganized asm and source folder layout while preserving build/link inputs.
Renamed from asm/code_80391580.s and adjusted the section address-range comment from the broad .text range to 0x80391580 - 0x8039F788.
New home for MSL assembly files such as abort_exit.s, ansi_fp.s, buffer_io.s, console_io.s, cstring.s, ctype.s, math.s, printf.s, string.s, strtoul.s, and wchar_io.s.
New home for MetroTRK assembly files such as dispatch.s, dolphin_trk.s, flush_cache.s, main_TRK.s, mainloop.s, mem_TRK.s, msg.s, and targimpl.s.
New subsystem layout for Dolphin SDK assembly, including ai, ar, ax, base, card, db, dsp, dvd, gx, hio, jpeg, mcc, mtx, os/init, pad, thp, and vi folders.
New folder for fighter assembly files such as ftmario.s, ftfox.s, ftkirby.s, ftlink.s, ftbosslib.s, and many other ft* files.
New folder for stage/ground assembly files such as grbattle.s, grbigblue.s, grcastle.s, grcorneria.s, grstadium.s, and other gr* files.
New folder for melee lb assembly; includes rename from asm/lbcommands_asm.s to asm/melee/lb/lbcommands.s.
New folder for baselib assembly, including AXDriver.s, archive.s, class.s, cobj.s, displayfunc.s, fobj.s, gobjobject.s, objalloc.s, and many other files.

what_changed: Reorganized flat ASM files into subsystem folders on the assembly side.
Preemptively created or populated folder structure intended to show future organization for Melee code.
Changed Makefile SRC_DIRS from a hard-coded list of source directories to `$(shell find src/ -type f -name '*.c')`.
Changed Makefile ASM_DIRS from `asm` to `$(shell find asm/ -type f -name '*.s')`, allowing recursive assembly discovery after files were moved into nested folders.
Reduced C_FILES from `$(wildcard src/*.c) $(wildcard src/sysdolphin/*.c)` to `$(wildcard src/*.c)` in the shown diff.
Updated Makefile include paths by adding `src/sysdolphin/baselib/` and replacing `src/melee/lib/` with `src/melee/lb/`.
Updated obj_files.mk object paths to match renamed folders, for example `src/runtime/__mem.o` became `src/Runtime/__mem.o` in INIT_O_FILES.
Moved MSL files from asm root into asm/MSL with 100% similarity renames.
Moved MetroTRK files from asm root into asm/MetroTRK with 100% similarity renames.
Moved Runtime files from asm root into asm/Runtime with 100% similarity renames.

Moved Dolphin SDK files into nested asm/dolphin subsystem folders such as asm/dolphin/ai/ai.s, asm/dolphin/ar/ar.s, asm/dolphin/ax/AX.s, asm/dolphin/base/PPCArch.s, asm/dolphin/card/CARD.s, asm/dolphin/dsp/dsp.s, asm/dolphin/gx/GXFifo.s, and asm/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.s.
Moved Melee fighter files into asm/melee/ft and ground/stage files into asm/melee/gr.

Removed `_asm` suffix from several relocated filenames, including `lbcommands_asm.s` to `lbcommands.s`, `displayfunc_asm.s` to `displayfunc.s`, `fobj_asm.s` to `fobj.s`, and `objalloc_asm.s` to `objalloc.s`.
Adjusted the section address comment in renamed `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/code_80391580.s` from `.section .text # 0x80005940 - 0x803B7240` to `.section .text # 0x80391580 - 0x8039F788`.
smart_moves: Kept most ASM relocations as 100% similarity renames, minimizing functional risk while changing the repository layout.
Updated obj_files.mk alongside the physical file moves, preserving build inputs after path changes.
Made assembly discovery recursive with `find asm/ -type f -name '*.s'`, which supports the new nested folder layout instead of requiring every asm file to stay at the root.
Grouped assembly by recognizable upstream/library boundaries: MSL, MetroTRK, Runtime, Dolphin SDK, Melee, and sysdolphin/baselib.
Renamed `_asm` suffixed files to cleaner module names once their path already distinguished them from C/source context.
Added the baselib include path in the same PR that introduced or emphasized the sysdolphin/baselib folder.
decomp_lessons: When moving large numbers of assembly files, preserve linker/object ordering explicitly in obj_files.mk; folder organization alone is not enough for a matching build.
Use directory structure to encode subsystem ownership instead of relying on ambiguous flat filenames.
For large-scale path cleanup, prefer pure renames with no content edits unless a small metadata fix is necessary; it keeps review and bisecting easier.
Recursive source/asm scanning can reduce Makefile churn after adding nested folders, but object lists still need to preserve exact link order where matching depends on it.
Once assembly and C files live in parallel subsystem folders, include paths should be updated immediately to match the new source hierarchy.
Removing `_asm` from filenames is clearer when the file location already indicates it is an assembly fallback or module implementation.
Section/address comments in assembly files should be narrowed when the file has a known code range; this improves future split/decomp navigation.
naming_conventions: Use top-level assembly subsystem folders with library-style capitalization where applicable: `asm/MSL`, `asm/MetroTRK`, and `asm/Runtime`.
Use nested Dolphin SDK subsystem paths under `asm/dolphin`, for example `asm/dolphin/gx/GXInit.s`, `asm/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.s`, and `asm/dolphin/pad/Padclamp.s`.
Use Melee subsystem prefixes as folders: `asm/melee/ft` for fighter files, `asm/melee/gr` for ground/stage files, and `asm/melee/lb` for lb files.

Prefer clean module filenames without `_asm` when the file path supplies context, as shown by `lbcommands_asm.s` to `lbcommands.s`, `displayfunc_asm.s` to `displayfunc.s`, `fobj_asm.s` to `fobj.s`, and `objalloc_asm.s` to `objalloc.s`.
Keep original module casing for known SDK/runtime names such as `NMWException.s`, `Gecko_setjmp.s`, `PPCArch.s`, `GXInit.s`, `GXFifo.s`, and `THPDec.s`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use 100% similarity git renames for large asm moves to demonstrate that code bytes/instructions were not intentionally changed.
After moving asm files, update every corresponding `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/...` object path in obj_files.mk rather than relying only on wildcard discovery.
Preserve object list order in TEXT_O_FILES and other object groups to avoid link-order changes that can break matching.
For split assembly ranges, keep `.section .text` address comments accurate to the file's covered address range, as in `code_80391580.s`.
Recursive Makefile scanning with `find` is useful after nested asm organization, but it should be paired with explicit object lists when the project depends on deterministic link order.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rely on the asm/MSL, asm/MetroTRK, asm/Runtime, asm/dolphin, asm/melee, or asm/sysdolphin/baselib folder structure.
Search for later fixes to Makefile SRC_DIRS or ASM_DIRS after this recursive scanning change.
Search for later PRs renaming `_asm` files or moving assembly into matching subsystem folders.
Check whether obj_files.mk ordering changes after this PR caused or fixed any matching/build issues.
Look for later work around `src/melee/lib` versus `src/melee/lb` include paths.
